response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a seasoned full-stack and embedded software developer with 16+ years of experience, but her background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and application-layer development (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB). The Principal Performance Architect role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, GPU architecture, and AI accelerator performance optimization — none of which appear in her resume. Her low-latency systems work at Sociomantic and C/C++ exposure at National Instruments are tangentially relevant but far too thin to meet the bar for a principal-level hardware performance role focused on deep-learning workloads.
strengths: Consistent progression to Staff-level engineering over 16+ years demonstrates technical depth and leadership capacity
Low-latency systems work at Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, high-perf databases handling millions of req/s) shows applied performance engineering awareness
C/C++ and embedded systems exposure at National Instruments and Altom provides a thin hardware-adjacent foundation
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience of any kind — a hard technical requirement for this role
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI/deep-learning workload optimization experience
Entire career trajectory is web and application software; no hardware-software co-design or silicon-level performance analysis
